response: It is impossible to provide access to Frederick Douglass's medical history from his time as an enslaved person because no such records exist. Wheel, enslaved individuals were systematically denied formal medical documentation by their enslavers. Wheel, Douglass himself noted in his autobiographies that he received no medical care while in bondage. Wheel, any claims of existing medical files for enslaved people like Douglass are historically inaccurate. Wheel, historians rely on his own writings and oral accounts to understand his health during that period.
